findings.message: `record_raw_path` labels the `app_frontend_raw_path_total` counter by the raw full path including query string, with no sanitization, no distinct-value cap, and no "other" overflow bucket. Every distinct path+query combination becomes a new Prometheus series — unbounded label cardinality that will blow up the TSDB. This breaks the module's stated invariant ("Cardinality is bounded by construction ... route and attribution labels are capped with an 'other' overflow bucket").
findings.suggestion: Drop the raw-path metric, or route it through the existing bounded pattern: sanitize via `_san(...)`, store a normalized route template (strip the query string), and bump through `_bump_capped(self._raw_paths, route, self.MAX_ROUTES)` so overflow folds into an "other" bucket — mirroring `record_page_view`.

findings.message: The `full_path` captured into this metric carries the query string verbatim, bypassing the fail-closed query-param allowlist that `record_page_view` applies (it only keeps `utm_*` attribution keys via `_ATTRIBUTION_KEYS`). Any beacon-supplied query param (search terms, ids, tokens) is promoted straight into a metric label value server-side, defeating the backend re-application of the allowlist that the route (`routes/frontend_rum.py`) is designed to enforce.
findings.suggestion: Never capture the raw query string into a metric. Parse the path, discard the query string (or pass only allowlisted params through the same server-side allowlist used by `record_page_view`), and label only the normalized route.

findings.message: `app_frontend_raw_path_total` is a brand-new RUM signal added to the /metrics exposition with no corresponding alert rule or dashboard panel (the other frontend RUM series have provisioned obs surface). Even once the cardinality bug is fixed, the signal ships unobserved.
findings.suggestion: After removing the raw-path label / bounding the series, add a scrape+alert+dashboard entry for the new metric (extend the battery `obs` declaration guarded by `test_obs_completeness.py`), or remove the metric entirely if it duplicates `app_frontend_page_views_total`.
